game_name: Double Dragon III: The Sacred Stones

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Early stages with manageable enemy pressure

element_introduction_order.element: Two-player cooperative techniques (back-to-back hurricane kick, triangle jump kick)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Emphasized when second player joins; requires positioning coordination

element_introduction_order.element: Item shop system (purchasable power-ups with additional credits)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Energy restores, Power Up increases attack speed; decision-making about resource allocation

element_introduction_order.element: Tricks unlock (solo hurricane kick, jumping throw)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Requires coin investment; expands combat toolkit mid-run

element_introduction_order.element: Extra fighter characters (Urquidez brothers - MMA fighters)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Alternative character types with different move sets; substitutes for traditional extra lives
Purchasable in shops; changes attack patterns and range
Specialized fighter type with tai chi move set
High-damage weapon for Lee brothers; signals increased difficulty tier
Specialized fighter type with karate techniques; mid-game character tier

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Overarching narrative (Egypt setting, three mystical stones)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Story-driven progression gate; directional markers for mission sequence

typical_relief_triggers: Item shop pause at stage start
Enemy group spacing in later missions
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Arcade-style escalating intensity with punctuated relief at shop interludes; constant low-to-moderate tension during combat with spiking pressure in later missions

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Item shop availability and purchasable character variants signal optional progression paths

difficulty_curve.early_game: Mission 1: Introduction to core combat and item shop mechanics; manageable enemy density for learning two-player cooperation
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Missions 2-3: Introduction of new fighter types (Chin, Ōyama), weapons (nunchaku, sword), and increased enemy pressure; resource management becomes critical
difficulty_curve.late_game: Missions 4-5: Reduced player damage advantage in overseas version (enemies deal full damage); sword availability; final Rosetta Stone retrieval with escalated difficulty
notable_spikes: Transition from Mission 1 to Mission 2 (enemy damage scaling introduced)
Mission 3 (sword weapon availability signals danger tier)
Mission 5 (final mission with full enemy AI and Urquidez brother availability for late-game character swaps)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each mission begins with optional shop access where players invest additional credits into power-ups, character unlocks, and special moves. Creates resource allocation tension and natural pacing breaks.
Players can hold up to three extra fighters in reserve and cycle between them mid-combat. Inherited moves persist; encourages strategic character rotation rather than traditional lives system.
Nunchaku in Mission 2 (moderate combat upgrade); Sword in Missions 3 and 5 (high-damage finisher). Weapon availability signals difficulty progression and stage-specific strategic options.
Back-to-back hurricane kick and triangle jump kick require two-player proximity and timing. Early introduction in Mission 1 but deepens difficulty as enemy group formations tighten in later stages.

sources_used: wikipedia - Double Dragon 3: The Rosetta Stone
